title: Authentik/OIDC login flow
description: Implement the OIDC authorization-code flow against Authentik: GET /auth/login redirects to the authorization endpoint, GET /auth/callback exchanges the code, validates the ID token, and establishes a session. Add a users table populated from the validated identity.
acceptance_criteria: GET /auth/login redirects to Authentik's authorization endpoint with correct client_id/redirect_uri/scope
GET /auth/callback exchanges the authorization code for tokens and validates the ID token signature/issuer/audience before trusting it
A users row (id, oidc_subject, email, display_name, created_at) is created on first login and matched by oidc_subject on subsequent logins
Invalid or tampered ID tokens are rejected, not silently accepted
auth.enabled=false skips all of the above and preserves current no-login behaviour exactly
No ownership, permission, or data-access change results from a successful login in this feature
notes: See docs/adr/0003-authentik-oidc-authentication-backend.md. users table exists for Phase 3 (EPIC-103) to attach ownership to later; nothing reads it for authorization purposes yet.
